intro:
I couldn't find any information about Tanacetum praeteritum. However, I can provide information on Tanacetum parthenium, also known as Feverfew, which is a plant in the same genus. 

Tanacetum parthenium, or Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Feverfew include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and migraines, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Feverfew include sesquiterpene lactones, such as parthenolide, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Feverfew include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of migraines and other conditions.
